records.ety: [L. crisis, Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/, fr. <?/<?/<?/<?/ to separate. See Certain.]

defs: The point of time when it is to be decided whether any affair or course of action must go on, or be modified or terminate; the decisive moment; the turning point.
quotes: This hour's the very crisis of your fate.
The very times of crisis for the fate of the country.

defs: That change in a disease which indicates whether the result is to be recovery or death; sometimes, also, a striking change of symptoms attended by an outward manifestation, as by an eruption or sweat.
quotes: Till some safe crisis authorize their skill.
